title: Response-Related Signals Increase Confidence But Not Metacognitive Performance.

assets.abstract: Confidence judgments are a central tool in metacognition research. In a typical task, participants first perform perceptual (first-order) decisions and then rate their confidence in these decisions. The relationship between confidence and first-order accuracy is taken as a measure of metacognitive performance. Confidence is often assumed to stem from decision-monitoring processes alone, but processes that co-occur with the first-order decision may also play a role in confidence formation. In fac

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.
